scenario: Vertex Bank rolls out Camunda 8.8 Tasklist and an operations manager triages 4500 tasks per day across loan origination and KYC reviews. She wants to know which preview attributes Tasklist lists on each task tile in the queue, so the design team can decide which process variables to surface as a context description for human reviewers.
question: Which set of fields does the Tasklist queue preview show on each task tile in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: Task name, process name, assignee, claimed date, completion date, business key, and SLA breach indicator displayed on every visible task tile
Task name, process name, optional context description, assignee, priority, creation date, due date, and follow up date on every task tile
Task name, candidate group, task version, last update date, due date, follow up date, process instance key, and tenant id per tile
Task name, bpmnProcessId, taskDefinitionId, assignee, last modified date, due date, claim count, and cluster identifier per tile

a.text: Incorrect. The documented tile fields are task name, process name, optional context description, assignee, priority, creation date, due date, and follow up date. Claimed date, completion date, business key, and SLA indicators are not part of the queue preview.

b.text: Correct. The Tasks queue section lists exactly task name, the name of the process the task belongs to, an optional context description, assignee, priority, creation date, due date, and follow up date as the queue tile preview fields.

c.text: Incorrect. Candidate group, task version, last update date, process instance key, and tenant id are not documented queue tile fields. The tile shows priority alongside the creation, due, and follow up dates.

d.text: Incorrect. bpmnProcessId, taskDefinitionId, last modified date, claim count, and cluster identifier are not part of the queue tile. The documented preview uses task name, process name, context description, assignee, priority, and three date fields.

explanation: The Tasklist tasks queue tile preview shows the task name, the name of the process the task belongs to, an optionally configured task context description, the assignee, the priority, the creation date, the due date, and the follow up date.
